title: May is National Mental Health Awareness Month, a time to fight the stigma surrounding mental health issues and illnesses by helping educate people on the services, support, and car

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has led to a sharp worsening of people’s mental health, especially among the young, unemployed, and those facing financial insecurity.",
    "Mental illness or disorders are a leading cause of disabilities in the United States.",
    "In 2019, 20% of adults in the United States experienced mental illness, most often depression or an anxiety disorder.",
    "More than half of Americans reported that COVID-19 had negatively affected their mental health by February 2021.",
    "45% of California youth between the ages of 12 and 17 reported having struggled recently with mental health issues in spring 2021.",
    "High school students with depression are twice as likely to drop out as their classmates.",
    "7 in 10 youth in the juvenile justice system have a mental health condition.",
    "There is a great need to improve public understanding of mental illness so that all those with mental illness may receive adequate and appropriate treatment.",
    "SB 1298 will continuously appropriate $1 billion in General Fund revenue annually to help counties build out mental health bed capacity.",
    "SB 1427 would create grant programs to help counties establish or expand collaborative mental health and homeless courts."
  ]
}
```
